'''Josh Thewlis''' (born 30 April 2002) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Thewlis was born in [[Grasscroft]], [[Oldham]], [[Greater Manchester]], England. He was educated at St Edwards’ Primary, Lees, and St Damian's RC Science College.<ref>[https://saddind.co.uk/thewlis-brothers-line-up-for-super-league-warrington/ Thewlis brothers line up for Super League Warrington]</ref>

His brother [[Jake Thewlis]] is a fellow Warrington Wolves player.

Played amateur rugby league for Waterhead Warriors.

He is of [[Irish people|Irish]] descent, representing Ireland against Wales at Under 16's level in 2017 (https://europeanrugbyleague.com/fixtures/817)

==Career==
===Warrington Wolves===
Thewlis made his Super League debut for Warrington in their 34–4 victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]] in round 17 of the [[Super League XXIV|2019 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/48515912 |title=Super League:Warrington Wolves v Catalans Dragons |publisher=BBC |date=6 June 2019}}</ref>
In round 8 of the 2023 Super League season, Thewlis scored two tries for Warrington in their 20–14 victory over Catalans.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65189717|title=Super League: Catalans Dragons 14-20 Warrington Wolves - Wire retain 100% record despite Dudson red|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Thewlis played 21 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He did not play in their elimination playoff defeat against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref> In October 2023 Thewlis was called up to the full [[England national rugby league team|England]] squad ahead of the third test in the [[2023 Tonga rugby league tour of England|series against Tonga]].<ref>{{cite web |title=England v Tonga: Hosts 'obsessed' with series clean sweep says head coach Shaun Wane |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67276014 |publisher=BBC Sport |access-date=3 November 2023 |date=31 October 2023}}</ref>
On 17 March 2024, Thewlis scored a [[hat-trick]] in Warrington's 58–4 victory over the hapless [[London Broncos|London]] side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68569783|title=London Broncos 4-58 Warrington Wolves: Two hat-tricks help Wire return to top spot|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 8 June 2024, Thewlis played in Warrington's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] defeat against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Thewlis played a total of 22 games for Warrington in the 2024 Super League season as the club reached the semi-final before losing to Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/24654682.warrington-wolves-2024-season-review-february-march/|title=Warrington Wolves 2024 season review, February and March|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025,Thewlis played in Warrington's 8-6 Challenge Cup final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Thewlis played 25 games for Warrington in the 2025 Super League season as Warrington missed the playoffs finishing 8th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
